prerequisites: An AWS account with an existing S3 bucket. You provide the bucket name in s3_bucket_name and its AWS region in s3_bucket_region.
A warehouse location for the Iceberg data, expressed as an S3 path of the form s3://<bucket-name>/path/within/bucket (warehouse_location).
A choice of Iceberg catalog backend for the catalog_type field. The connector supports AWS Glue, Nessie, REST, and Polaris (Apache Polaris) catalogs.

AWS credentials with permission to read and write objects in the target bucket. For IAM access key authentication you supply access_key_id and secret_access_key; on AWS Glue you may instead rely on an attached IAM role. The connector needs S3 actions such as s3:ListBucket, s3:GetObject, s3:PutObject, and s3:DeleteObject on the bucket, plus Glue table/database actions (for example glue:CreateTable, glue:UpdateTable, glue:GetTable) when using the AWS Glue catalog.
A primary Iceberg branch name for writes (main_branch_name).
If you target an S3-compatible store rather than AWS S3, the custom endpoint URL (s3_endpoint), which is optional.

authMethods.name: AWS IAM access key (access_key_id / secret_access_key)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate to S3 with an IAM user access key pair. Access keys consist of an access key ID and a secret access key that must be used together to sign requests. Create the key in the IAM console under the user's Security credentials; the secret access key is shown only once at creation, so save it securely. These map to the connector's access_key_id and secret_access_key fields. Both are optional in the descriptor because, with the AWS Glue catalog, the connector can fall back to an attached IAM role instead of static keys.

authMethods.name: Nessie catalog access token (catalog_type.access_token)
authMethods.summary: When catalog_type is Nessie, the connector authenticates to the Nessie catalog service using a bearer access token supplied in catalog_type.access_token, alongside the catalog URI. S3 object access still uses the AWS credentials above.

authMethods.name: Polaris OAuth client credentials (catalog_type.client_id / catalog_type.client_secret)
authMethods.summary: When catalog_type is Apache Polaris, the connector authenticates to Polaris with an OAuth client ID and client secret obtained when creating a Polaris principal. These map to catalog_type.client_id and catalog_type.client_secret. S3 object access still uses the AWS credentials above.

setupSteps.title: Create or identify the S3 bucket and warehouse path
setupSteps.body: In AWS, use an existing S3 bucket or create one. Note its name (s3_bucket_name) and region (s3_bucket_region). Decide where Iceberg data should live and set warehouse_location to an S3 path such as s3://<bucket-name>/path/within/bucket. If you are writing to an S3-compatible store instead of AWS S3, set s3_endpoint to that store's URL.

setupSteps.title: Create AWS credentials with S3 access
setupSteps.body: Create an IAM user (or role) that can read and write the target bucket, granting S3 actions such as s3:ListBucket, s3:GetObject, s3:PutObject, and s3:DeleteObject scoped to the bucket. For access key authentication, open the IAM console, go to the user's Security credentials, and create an access key. Copy the access key ID into access_key_id and the secret access key into secret_access_key. The secret is displayed only once at creation, so store it immediately. With the AWS Glue catalog you may instead attach an IAM role and leave the key fields empty.

setupSteps.title: Choose and configure the Iceberg catalog
setupSteps.body: Set catalog_type to your catalog backend. For AWS Glue, also grant Glue actions such as glue:CreateTable, glue:UpdateTable, and glue:GetTable. For a Nessie catalog, provide the catalog URI and a bearer token in catalog_type.access_token. For Apache Polaris, create a principal and supply its OAuth client_id and client_secret in catalog_type.client_id and catalog_type.client_secret. For a REST catalog, supply the catalog URI. Set main_branch_name to the primary Iceberg branch to write to.

setupSteps.title: Set optional tuning and save the connection
setupSteps.body: Optionally adjust flush_batch_size_mb to control the batch size at which buffered records are flushed and published. Enter all required fields (catalog_type, s3_bucket_name, s3_bucket_region, warehouse_location, main_branch_name) plus the credentials, then save and test the destination.
